message:
perf(db): budget-aware PRAGMA tuning for high-concurrency workloads (br-15dv.1.6)

Key changes to SQLite PRAGMA settings for 1000+ agent workloads:

- cache_size: dynamic per-connection budget (512MB total / max_connections),
  clamped to [2MB, 64MB]. Prevents memory blowup at high pool sizes.
  Was fixed 64MB/conn (6.4GB with 100 connections).
- mmap_size: reduced from 512MB to 256MB per connection. OS shares pages
  across connections via the page cache, so 512MB was excessive.
- journal_size_limit: added 64MB cap to prevent unbounded WAL growth
  during sustained write bursts.
- build_conn_pragmas(max_connections): new function generates budget-aware
  per-connection PRAGMAs; used by DbPool::new() instead of static constant.

Added 2 tests validating budget scaling and zero-pool fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
